references.title: Prerequisite-Driven Deep Knowledge Tracing
references.journal: IEEE International Conference on Data Mining

extracted.Student Performance Definition: NExt Candidate Question is Answerewd Correctly
extracted.Students: 1610 ASSISTments learners, 2648 AICFE-CM learners, 1451 AICFE-GE learners, 2445 AICFE-MA learners, 964 AICFE-PH learners
extracted.Data sources: Sequence of Questions, Sequence of Answers
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models: The idea of prerequisite knowledge between different knowledge concepts is underexplored in common KT frameworks.
extracted.Novelty of Model: Model proposed treats prequesities ar ordering pairs. Essencially, the probability of mastering a concept needs to be lower than the probability of having mastered concepts that are prequisites.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
The first step starts with the building of 2 matrices:

A student-question matrix indicating whether the question was correctly or incorrectly answered.

A Question-Concept Mapping intending to identify which concepts are associated with each question.

A set of pre-requisite relationships derived from ppedagogical experts. 

Modelling of these constraints is attempted in different forms:

1. Logic, 2. Boolean Factors and 3. A Conditional Probability, 4. An Ordering Pair
extracted.Comments:
The main difference of this approach relative to DKT is that it includes constraints on pre-requisite knowledge.

On all datasets, the authors show that PDKT-C is the most effective method out of all tested across all datasets.

Authors also do an Ablation study to check the effect of different parameter changes to model performance: 
-Hidden Units, Lambda, Trainig dataset size, Number oif considered pre-requisites.
extracted.Limitations:
Does not consider additional sources of data,

Pre-requisite extraction needs optimization.

annotations.text: One of the main bottlenecks to the deployment of KT approaches is the sparseness in exercise data. To circumvent this issue,. the authors propose that KT models could incorporate pre-requequisites into the KT model. Authors introduce pre-requisites in the form of a constraint and show, on 5 datasets, that this alteration improves the standard KT approaches.
